seo.title: Building Content Generation with Mastra + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build content generation with Mastra + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building content generation agents with Mastra enables scalable LLM-powered workflows that enforce brand voice, fact-check outputs, and adhere to editorial guidelines. However, without proper guardrails, these agents risk producing inconsistent branding, factual errors, or SEO-violating content that damages reputation. Vex integration as middleware ensures reliable outputs through automated verification at every pipeline stage.

architectureOverview.heading: Content Generation Architecture with Mastra
architectureOverview.description:
Mastra orchestrates content generation through sequential workflow steps: research → drafting → brand voice alignment → fact-checking → SEO optimization → final review. Each step leverages tools for web search, knowledge retrieval, and LLM processing, with memory systems maintaining brand guidelines and editorial context across iterations.

The pipeline uses Mastra's AgentExecutor to chain LLM calls with custom tools, injecting retrieved knowledge and previous step outputs. Critical transitions occur between drafting (creative generation) and verification (brand/fact-checking), where malformed data or stale context can cascade failures. Vex middleware intercepts outputs at these transitions for validation.

Memory retrieval pulls brand voice examples and style guides, while third-party integrations handle plagiarism checks and SEO analysis. The architecture's strength lies in its modularity, but this introduces risks around data validation between workflow nodes.

risks.name: Workflow step outputs not validated between transitions
risks.description: Drafting step produces unstructured text that confuses brand alignment LLM, leading to voice drift
risks.mitigation: Vex middleware parses and validates JSON-structured outputs before workflow advancement

risks.name: Agent tool calls returning malformed data
risks.description: Web search tools return unparsed HTML snippets instead of clean facts, poisoning fact-checking
risks.mitigation: Vex tool response validators extract structured data from raw tool outputs

risks.name: Memory retrieval injecting stale context
risks.description: Outdated brand guidelines or expired SEO keywords retrieved, causing compliance violations
risks.mitigation: Vex memory freshness checks and content timestamp validation

risks.name: Sync integration data causing agent confusion
risks.description: Plagiarism checker returns binary pass/fail without context, confusing editorial LLM
risks.mitigation: Vex normalizes third-party API responses into consistent JSON schemas
